tossups.question: In 1992, autobiographical writings by these people were collected in Poisoned Bread and an anthology co-edited by Eleanor Zelliot. A modernist novel is titled for one of these people who prizes a hockey stick and the “fashun” of Tommies from the British barracks. Latin American testimonios inspired the writings of women from this group like Bama and the author of The Prisons We Broke. An uncle and nephew from this group ride the morning express in the prologue of a novel in which they work as tailors for Dina and are later sterilized. One of these people named Bakha titles a 1935 novel by Mulk Raj Anand. A Communist member of this group who works at Paradise Pickles & Preserves is beaten to death after he is blamed for the murder of Sophie Mol by Baby Kochamma. For 10 points, Ammu transgresses the “Love Laws” with a member of what caste named Velutha in The God of Small Things?
tossups.answer: Dalits [or Dalita; accept Untouchables; accept Scheduled Castes or SCs; accept Chamar or Jatav; accept An Anthology of Dalit Literature; prompt on outcastes; prompt on Malayali, Keralite, Marāṭhī, Indian people, sweepers, or latrine cleaners by asking “of what class?”] (Baby Kamble wrote The Prisons We Broke. The fourth sentence is from A Fine Balance by Rohinton Mistry. Anand’s novel is Untouchable. Arundhati Roy wrote The God of Small Things.)

packet.name: Prelims 4. Claremont A, McGill A, Stanford A

tossups.answer_sanitized: Dalits [or Dalita; accept Untouchables; accept Scheduled Castes or SCs; accept Chamar or Jatav; accept An Anthology of Dalit Literature; prompt on outcastes; prompt on Malayali, Keralite, Marathi, Indian people, sweepers, or latrine cleaners by asking "of what class?"] (Baby Kamble wrote The Prisons We Broke. The fourth sentence is from A Fine Balance by Rohinton Mistry. Anand's novel is Untouchable. Arundhati Roy wrote The God of Small Things.)

tossups.question: An Indian-born immigrant to this country included a scene in which a helicopter causes a giant cutout of a leader to topple on civilians in a novel about the tailor Dina Dalal set during the Emergency. A forensic pathologist discovers a recently-buried skeleton in a novel from this country titled Anil’s Ghost. While stealing military documents, a morphine-addicted thief from this country is caught and has his thumbs chopped off, inspiring his name (*) Caravaggio. A copy of Herodotus’s Histories leads the injured title character of a novel from this country to recall his past experiences while being cared for by the nurse Hana in a bombed-out Italian villa during World War II. For 10 points, what country is home to the Sri Lankan-born author of The English Patient, Michael Ondaatje (“on-DAH-chee”)?
tossups.answer: Canada (The first novel is Rohinton Mistry’s A Fine Balance.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: Canada (The first novel is Rohinton Mistry's A Fine Balance.)
tossups.question_sanitized: An Indian-born immigrant to this country included a scene in which a helicopter causes a giant cutout of a leader to topple on civilians in a novel about the tailor Dina Dalal set during the Emergency. A forensic pathologist discovers a recently-buried skeleton in a novel from this country titled Anil's Ghost. While stealing military documents, a morphine-addicted thief from this country is caught and has his thumbs chopped off, inspiring his name (*) Caravaggio. A copy of Herodotus's Histories leads the injured title character of a novel from this country to recall his past experiences while being cared for by the nurse Hana in a bombed-out Italian villa during World War II. For 10 points, what country is home to the Sri Lankan-born author of The English Patient, Michael Ondaatje ("on-DAH-chee")?

tossups.question: In the epilogue to this novel, a character weeps upon recognizing three girls from his youth in a newspaper article reporting that they had hanged themselves because their father could not afford their dowries. To maximize public sympathy, a “Beggarmaster” in this novel manages a troupe of people with diverse deformities, such as being legless or having no eyes. In its climactic scene, two of this novel’s main characters are piled into a truck and taken to the Family (*) Planning Center, where one has his legs amputated and the other is castrated after both undergo compulsory vasectomies. To remain financially independent, a woman in this novel takes on the paying boarder Maneck Kohlah and works the untouchable tailors Ishvar and Omprakash under sweatshop conditions. For 10 points, name this set at the apartment of the Parsi woman Dina Dilal, the best-known novel by Canadian-Indian novelist Rohinton Mistry.
tossups.question_sanitized: In the epilogue to this novel, a character weeps upon recognizing three girls from his youth in a newspaper article reporting that they had hanged themselves because their father could not afford their dowries. To maximize public sympathy, a "Beggarmaster" in this novel manages a troupe of people with diverse deformities, such as being legless or having no eyes. In its climactic scene, two of this novel's main characters are piled into a truck and taken to the Family (*) Planning Center, where one has his legs amputated and the other is castrated after both undergo compulsory vasectomies. To remain financially independent, a woman in this novel takes on the paying boarder Maneck Kohlah and works the untouchable tailors Ishvar and Omprakash under sweatshop conditions. For 10 points, name this set at the apartment of the Parsi woman Dina Dilal, the best-known novel by Canadian-Indian novelist Rohinton Mistry.

bonuses.leadin: At the end of a novel titled for these people, a boy named Bakha rushes home to tell his father about the introduction of the flush toilet to their country. For 10 points each:
parts: Name these people. In The God of Small Things, a Communist one of these people named Velutha dies in prison after Estha and Rahel tell Inspector Thomas Mathew that he had murdered their cousin Sophie Mol.

Balram Halwai describes his untouchability as “the Darkness” to Wen Jiabao in an Aravind Adiga novel titled for a white one of these animals. One of these animals named Richard Parker accompanies the title character in Life of Pi.
The untouchable Ishvar has his legs amputated and his nephew Om is castrated because they disrespect an upper-class gangster at the end of this novel set during the Emergency.
answers: Dalits [accept untouchables] (The first novel is Untouchable by Mulk Raj Anand.)
answers_sanitized: Dalits [accept untouchables] (The first novel is Untouchable by Mulk Raj Anand.)
tigers [accept The White Tiger; or Bengal tiger; or Panthera tigris tigris]

Balram Halwai describes his untouchability as "the Darkness" to Wen Jiabao in an Aravind Adiga novel titled for a white one of these animals. One of these animals named Richard Parker accompanies the title character in Life of Pi.
The untouchable Ishvar has his legs amputated and his nephew Om is castrated because they disrespect an upper-class gangster at the end of this novel set during the Emergency.

bonuses.leadin: In this novel, two characters notice that a billboard for Modern Bread has been replaced by a picture of the prime minister. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel, in which Dina Dalal forges an inseparable relationship with the Darji tailors and her tenant Maneck Kohlah amid the turmoil of the Emergency.

In this other novel, the “magician’s ghetto” of Picture Singh is destroyed as part of the Emergency’s policies. Saleem Sinai is forced to undergo a vasectomy by Emergency officials in this Salman Rushdie novel.

After visiting India during the Emergency, this author wrote the second book in his Indian Trilogy. The pundit Jayaram harasses the protagonist of another of his novels, who was born “in the wrong way” and with six fingers.
answers: V. S. Naipaul [or Vidiadhar Surajprasad Naipaul] (The second novel is A House for Mr. Biswas.)

packet.name: A - Berkeley A, Waterloo B, CWRU A, Rutgers B, WUSTL C

answers_sanitized: V. S. Naipaul [or Vidiadhar Surajprasad Naipaul] (The second novel is A House for Mr. Biswas.)
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: In this novel, two characters notice that a billboard for Modern Bread has been replaced by a picture of the prime minister. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this novel, in which Dina Dalal forges an inseparable relationship with the Darji tailors and her tenant Maneck Kohlah amid the turmoil of the Emergency.

In this other novel, the "magician's ghetto" of Picture Singh is destroyed as part of the Emergency's policies. Saleem Sinai is forced to undergo a vasectomy by Emergency officials in this Salman Rushdie novel.

After visiting India during the Emergency, this author wrote the second book in his Indian Trilogy. The pundit Jayaram harasses the protagonist of another of his novels, who was born "in the wrong way" and with six fingers.

bonuses.leadin: This novel’s protagonist, who sweeps latrines for a living, is delighted to hear about the prospect of flush toilets coming to India. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this 1935 novel by Mulk Raj Anand that ends with Bakha watching an anti-discrimination speech by Mahatma Gandhi. It was one of the first major Indian novels written in English.

Arundhati Roy has received flak for claiming that Gandhi accepted caste discrimination and calling for institutions named after him to be renamed. The mother of the protagonists has an affair with an untouchable employee named Velutha in this novel, her first and best known.

A third English-language novel about untouchables is this novel set during the Emergency by Rohinton Mistry. This novel ends with Omprakash being forcibly castrated after disrespecting an upper caste gangster.
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This novel's protagonist, who sweeps latrines for a living, is delighted to hear about the prospect of flush toilets coming to India. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this 1935 novel by Mulk Raj Anand that ends with Bakha watching an anti-discrimination speech by Mahatma Gandhi. It was one of the first major Indian novels written in English.

Arundhati Roy has received flak for claiming that Gandhi accepted caste discrimination and calling for institutions named after him to be renamed. The mother of the protagonists has an affair with an untouchable employee named Velutha in this novel, her first and best known.

A third English-language novel about untouchables is this novel set during the Emergency by Rohinton Mistry. This novel ends with Omprakash being forcibly castrated after disrespecting an upper caste gangster.

bonuses.leadin: In this novel’s epilogue, Maneck commits suicide by letting himself get run over by a train. For 10 points each:
parts: Dina Dalal illegally hires two tailors to work in her home in this novel. One of those tailors, Ishvar, loses both his legs to gangrene during a castration procedure ordered by Thakur.

Rohinton Mistry’s A Fine Balance chronologically begins at this event. Two people born during this event are switched at birth and are distinguished by enormous knees and telepathic ability in Midnight’s Children.
Midnight’s Children is a novel by this British-Indian author, who described the effects of the fatwa imposed on him after he published The Satanic Verses in his recent memoir Joseph Anton.
answers: India’s Independence [accept logical equivalents, as well as Partition of India]
answers_sanitized: India's Independence [accept logical equivalents, as well as Partition of India]
